question_title: Which range will the Henry Hub natural gas spot price (USD per MMBtu) fall in on April 30, 2026?
summary: Henry Hub natural gas spot price on April 30, 2026 is most likely to fall in the $2.50-$3.50 range. Current spot is ~$3.00 (FRED, March 2), futures for April/May 2026 trade at ~$3.19-$3.20, and last year's April 30 spot was $3.12 — all firmly in this range. Spring shoulder season typically brings moderate prices between winter heating and summer cooling demand. Monte Carlo simulations with ~50-55% annualized volatility and fat-tailed returns (t-distribution, df=5) centered on the futures-implied price consistently assign ~55% probability to the 2.50-3.50 bucket, ~28% to 3.50-4.50 (upside from weather/supply risks), ~11% to below 2.50 (downside from oversupply/mild weather), and ~6% to 4.50+ (extreme tail events).

factors.description: Futures curve anchoring: May 2026 NG futures at $3.20 and Apr 2026 at $3.19 — market expects price firmly in 2.50-3.50 range
Current spot price at ~$3.00 (FRED March 2) — well within 2.50-3.50 range, status quo favors this bucket
Spring shoulder season typically brings lower prices as heating demand drops — seasonal downward pressure from winter highs
EIA STEO forecast 2026 annual avg ~$3.50, revised up 23% after Winter Storm Fern — but actual spot already well below March forecast of $4.12
Natural gas extreme volatility (~50-65% annualized) creates meaningful tail risk — Monte Carlo shows ~11% chance of falling below $2.50
Late cold snap, supply disruption, or LNG demand surge could push prices into $3.50-4.50 range — Monte Carlo shows ~28% probability
LNG export ramp (Golden Pass, Plaquemines) adds structural demand support that could tighten market beyond futures expectations
April 30, 2025 spot price was $3.12 — historical precedent supports 2.50-3.50 range for late April
Extreme upside scenario ($4.50+) requires major supply disruption or extreme late-season cold — low probability tail event

reasoning:
**Forecast: Henry Hub Natural Gas Spot Price on April 30, 2026**

| Range | Probability |
|-------|------------|
| Below $2.50 | 11% |
| **$2.50 to below $3.50** | **55%** |
| $3.50 to below $4.50 | 28% |
| $4.50 or higher | 6% |

**Key reasoning:**
- **Current spot** is ~$3.00 (FRED, March 2) and the **futures curve** prices April/May 2026 at ~$3.19-$3.20, both firmly in the $2.50-$3.50 range
- **Spring shoulder season** brings lower prices as heating demand drops — last year's April 30 spot was $3.12
- Natural gas has **~50-65% annualized volatility**, creating real tail risk in both directions over 40 trading days
- Monte Carlo simulations (200k paths, t-distribution with fat tails) centered on the futures-implied target robustly assign the majority of probability to $2.50-$3.50, with meaningful upside risk from weather events or supply disruptions
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) Which range will the Henry Hub natural gas spot price (USD per MMBtu) fall in on April 30, 2026?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42502)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves to the option whose range contains the “Henry Hub Natural Gas Spot Price (Dollars per Million Btu)” value for April 30, 2026 as published before May 1, 2026 on FRED series DHHNGSP at https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/DHHNGSP
fine_print: If April 30, 2026 has no observation, use the most recent prior date with an observation. Use the value shown on FRED. Candidate Sources: EIA Henry Hub spot price history (backup) https://www.eia.gov/dnav/ng/hist/rngwhhdD.htm | Investing.com Natural Gas historical data (backup) https://www.investing.com/commodities/natural-gas-historical-data
background_info:
This forecasts US benchmark gas pricing, relevant for power markets, LNG economics, and inflation-sensitive energy components.
